steps.title: Identify the grammatical structures
steps.content:
Before the blank: “To humans, it does not appear that the golden orb-weaver spider uses camouflage to capture its prey” is an independent clause.
After the blank: “the brightly colored arachnid seems to wait conspicuously in the center of its large circular web for insects to approach” is an independent clause.

steps.title: Apply the rule for conjunctive adverbs
steps.content:
A semicolon before 'rather' and a comma after it correctly joins two independent clauses with a conjunctive adverb.

The other options fail by using only commas, lacking punctuation between clauses, or placing the semicolon after 'rather' instead of before it.

This is the correct answer.
